content.boundary: Applies to clusters of village primary schools within a defined micro-region under 2,000-inhabitant settlements; does not require municipal re-acquisition of maintainer status, and building repurposing applies to non-classroom space only.
content.change_lever: Formal inter-municipal pedagogical networks (shared staffing, joint curriculum planning, rotating specialist teachers across a cluster of village schools) combined with legal/regulatory permission for schools to co-locate with other community services (crèche, library, health outreach), following French RPI and Polish 2025 draft small-schools legislation models.
content.name: Networked retention: inter-municipal school clusters and multi-service community hubs
content.system_problem: Individual small village schools facing sub-threshold enrollment are treated as isolated units subject to closure under national class-size/funding formulas, when clustering resources across several villages or repurposing school buildings for combined civic functions could sustain viability without requiring full closure or full independent operation.
